verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1 beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, canonically located in the mitochondrial matrix-facing catalytic head with no TM domain or GPI anchor. However, there is well-documented 'ectopic' surface display of the ATP synthase beta subunit on the outer leaflet of tumor cells and endothelial cells, where it functions as a receptor for angiostatin and is detected by surface biotinylation and antibody binding on intact live cells. This constitutes cell-state-induced (oncogenic/angiogenic context) outer-leaflet exposure. The canonical compartment is mitochondrial-internal, but the documented tumor/endothelial surface pool qualifies as contextual surface accessibility under cell_state_induced.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Mechanism of PM translocation for ATP5F1B is incompletely understood; surface pool size and generality across tumor types is variable, and some reports may conflate subunits.
